name: Aviation Fuel EHS Mixture Reporting
description: The Springfield Regional Airport Authority has installed a new 10,000-gallon underground storage tank for Aviation Gasoline (Avgas 100LL). Because Avgas 100LL contains Tetraethyl Lead (an Extremely Hazardous Substance), you must report this mixture correctly. Using the EPA Tier2 Submit application, open the baseline facility file located at C:\Users\Docker\Documents\airport_base.t2s. Add a new chemical named 'Avgas 100LL'. Mark it as a 'Mixture' and flag the parent chemical as 'EHS'. Add its four components with their CAS numbers and weight percentages: Isooctane (CAS 540-84-1, 74.9%), Isopentane (CAS 78-78-4, 15%), Toluene (CAS 108-88-3, 10%), and Tetraethyl Lead (CAS 78-00-2, 0.1%). Specifically flag Tetraethyl Lead as an EHS component. Select the following hazards: Flammable (gases, aerosols, liquids, or solids), Carcinogenicity, and Acute toxicity (any route of exposure). Set the Maximum Daily Amount to 60000, Average Daily Amount to 45000, and Days On-Site to 365. Add a storage location: Type = Underground tank, Pressure = Ambient pressure, Temperature = Ambient temperature, and Location Description = 'General Aviation Fuel Farm - Tank 4'. Once complete, save and export your submission to C:\Users\Docker\Documents\airport_updated.t2s.
